message.content: Add a --verbose / -v flag to the CLI that shows debug logging. Should work with all subcommands.

content.text: I'll add verbose as a global option on the root command, then add a `debug` method to the logger that's gated on it.

content.content:
✓ logger
  ✓ info logs to stdout (1ms)
  ✓ error logs to stderr (1ms)
  ✓ debug is silent by default (1ms)
  ✓ debug logs when verbose enabled (2ms)
✓ cli
  ✓ --verbose flag enables debug output (5ms)

Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  5 passed (5)
Duration  0.67s

content.text:
Done. `--verbose` / `-v` is a global flag that works with all subcommands:

```bash
acme build -v        # debug logging on
acme dev --verbose   # same
acme test            # silent debug
```

Implementation: `commander`'s `preAction` hook checks the flag before any subcommand runs and enables the logger's debug method.

summary: Added global --verbose/-v flag using commander preAction hook. Debug logging gated behind verbose flag across all subcommands.
